sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Suspicious New Instance Of An Office COM Object
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description:
Detects an svchost process spawning an instance of an office application. This happens when the initial word application creates an instance of one of the Office COM objects such as 'Word.Application', 'Excel.Application', etc.
This can be used by malicious actors to create malicious Office documents with macros on the fly. (See vba2clr project in the references)

crowdsourced_yara_results.description: This signature identifies Adobe Extensible Metadata Platform (XMP) identifiers embedded within files. Defined as a standard for mapping graphical asset relationships, XMP allows for tracking of both parent-child relationships and individual revisions. There are three categories of identifiers: original document, document, and instance. Generally, XMP data is stored in XML format, updated on save/copy, and embedded within the graphical asset. These identifiers can be used to track both malicious and benign graphics within common Microsoft and Adobe document lures.
crowdsourced_yara_results.author: InQuest Labs

crowdsourced_ai_results.analysis:
Package: mitre-mcp-server (whl)
Version: 0.1.2
Description: MCP server providing access to MITRE ATT&CK threat intelligence framework

The `mitre-mcp-server` package functions as an AI-native interface to the MITRE ATT&CK threat intelligence framework. Its core functionality involves downloading official MITRE ATT&CK STIX data (Enterprise, Mobile, ICS) from trusted sources using the `mitreattack-python` library, storing it in a user-configurable local directory (defaulting to `~/.mitre-mcp-server/data`), and then serving this data through a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server. The package exposes numerous tools for querying techniques, groups, software, mitigations, campaigns, data sources, and generating ATT&CK Navigator layers. All network and file system operations are directly related to its stated purpose, and the use of hash verification during data download enhances integrity. The code demonstrates appropriate input validation for specific tools (e.g., `generate_layer`). No malicious or suspicious code patterns, unauthorized access attempts, or unexpected behaviors were identified.

crowdsourced_ids_results.rule_raw: alert dns $HOME_NET any -> any any (msg:"ET INFO File Hosting Service Domain Domain in DNS Lookup (files .pythonhosted .org)"; dns.query; bsize:22; content:"files.pythonhosted.org"; nocase; classtype:misc-activity; sid:2049201; rev:1; metadata:attack_target Client_Endpoint, created_at 2023_11_15, deployment Perimeter, performance_impact Low, confidence High, signature_severity Informational, tag Description_Generated_By_Proofpoint_Nexus, updated_at 2023_11_15, reviewed_at 2024_04_15;)

crowdsourced_ai_results.analysis:
Package: mantis-models (whl)
Version: 3.4.11
Description: M&NTIS library containing models used in M&NTIS components

The `mantis-models` package primarily consists of Pydantic data models and associated utility functions for defining structures related to cybersecurity scenarios, red teaming, and evaluations. It includes models for notifications, assessment results, infrastructure, attacks, scenarios, lab configurations, asset reports, security alerts, signatures, and common data types like IOCs and CPEs. The code handles data validation, serialization, and deserialization using standard libraries and well-known data modeling tools (Pydantic, ruamel.yaml). File system interactions are limited to reading and writing configuration and data files (YAML, JSON). A script `generate_mitre_json.py` is present for generating MITRE ATT&CK data, which is a build-time utility and not part of the package's runtime malicious behavior. There is no evidence of unauthorized network communication, malicious file system manipulation, privilege escalation, or other harmful activities. The package appears to be a legitimate library for data modeling within a cybersecurity context.

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Office Application Initiated Network Connection To Non-Local IP
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description:
Detects an office application (Word, Excel, PowerPoint)  that initiate a network connection to a non-private IP addresses.
This rule aims to detect traffic similar to one seen exploited in CVE-2021-42292.
This rule will require an initial baseline and tuning that is specific to your organization.
sigma_analysis_results.rule_author: Christopher Peacock '@securepeacock', SCYTHE '@scythe_io', Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Tim Shelton, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)

trid.file_type: Excel Microsoft Office Open XML Format document

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Execution of Suspicious File Type Extension
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description:
Detects whether the image specified in a process creation event doesn't refer to an ".exe" (or other known executable extension) file. This can be caused by process ghosting or other unorthodox methods to start a process.
This rule might require some initial baselining to align with some third party tooling in the user environment.

Detects modification of the registry settings used for Internet Explorer and other Windows components that use these settings. An attacker can abuse this registry key to add a domain to the trusted sites Zone or insert javascript for persistence
